title: Shared Volume: Log Shipper Sidecar
description:
Create a Pod named "logger-pod" where one container generates logs and a sidecar container reads them in real-time.

**Focus**: Using `spec.volumes` (emptyDir) and `volumeMounts` with different mount paths in each container.

This exercise demonstrates the "Log Shipper" sidecar pattern - a common production pattern where a helper container processes, aggregates, or forwards logs from the main application. In real-world scenarios, the sidecar might be Fluentd or Logstash shipping logs to Elasticsearch.

**The Exercise Setup**:
- **App Container**: Writes "Running..." to `/var/log/app.log` every 5 seconds
- **Sidecar Container**: Reads from `/logs/app.log` and streams to stdout using `tail -f`
- **Shared Volume**: An `emptyDir` volume mounted at different paths in each container

**Why Different Mount Paths?**
This demonstrates that the same volume can be accessed from different paths:
- App writes to `/var/log/app.log`
- Sidecar reads from `/logs/app.log`
- Both paths point to the same file in the shared volume

**Real-World Pattern**:
```yaml
# Production example with Fluentd sidecar
- name: app
  volumeMounts:
  - name: logs
    mountPath: /var/log
- name: fluentd
  image: fluent/fluentd:latest
  volumeMounts:
  - name: logs
    mountPath: /var/log  # Reads from same location
```
steps: Create app container that writes logs to /var/log/app.log
Mount log-storage volume to /var/log in app container
Create sidecar container that reads from /logs/app.log
Mount log-storage volume to /logs in sidecar container
Verify sidecar can read logs written by app container
hints: emptyDir creates a temporary directory shared between containers in the same Pod
Each container can mount the same volume to different paths
Use volumeMounts in each container to specify where the volume appears
The args field uses array syntax for shell commands

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: logger-pod
spec:
  volumes:
  - name: log-storage
    emptyDir: {}
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox:latest
    volumeMounts:
    - name: log-storage
      mountPath: /var/log
    command: ['/bin/sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo Running... >> /var/log/app.log; sleep 5; done']
  - name: sidecar
    image: busybox:latest
    volumeMounts:
    - name: log-storage
      mountPath: /logs
    # TODO: Add command to tail the log file at /logs/app.log

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: logger-pod
spec:
  volumes:
  - name: log-storage
    emptyDir: {}
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox
    volumeMounts:
    - name: log-storage
      mountPath: /var/log
    args:
    - /bin/sh
    - -c
    - 'while true; do echo "Running..." >> /var/log/app.log; sleep 5; done'
  - name: sidecar
    image: busybox
    volumeMounts:
    - name: log-storage
      mountPath: /logs
    args:
    - /bin/sh
    - -c
    - 'tail -f /logs/app.log'
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Apply the manifest
kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for pod to be ready
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod/logger-pod --timeout=60s

# Wait a few seconds for logs to be generated
sleep 10

# Check logs of the sidecar container
logs=$(kubectl logs logger-pod -c sidecar --tail=5)

if echo "$logs" | grep -q "Running..."; then
  echo "✓ Sidecar successfully reading logs from shared volume"
  exit 0
else
  echo "✗ Sidecar not reading logs. Output: $logs"
  exit 1
fi
